channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Mariah The Scientist Gets Real About Love, Pain & Her Creative Process With Billboard & Honda Stage
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to this special episode, where we're shining the spotlight on rising star, Mariah The Scientist. Recently, she sat down with Billboard and Honda Stage to talk about her creative journey, vulnerability, and connection with her fans.
[Soft music transitions in]
Host: Mariah, who was once a scientist, made a bold career change to pursue her passion for music. She believes that songwriting is an art form that allows her to create something from scratch. Her song "Rainy Days" is a prime example of her vulnerability, where she opens up about her personal struggles.
[Clip of Mariah The Scientist speaking]
Mariah The Scientist: "Vulnerability is very expensive... Honestly, to make a good song, you wind up exploiting your trauma, but it becomes rewarding at the end, once you do 1,000 shows, and you go and you see people crying and singing it because it makes you feel like you did something that people can connect to..."
Host: Mariah's connection with her fans is truly special. She considers them friends, and her music has become a way for them to feel seen and heard. When she's on stage, she's often overcome with emotion, and it's not uncommon for her to tear up when she hears her fans singing along to her songs.
[Clip of Mariah The Scientist speaking]
Mariah The Scientist: "I'll be trying not to do that, though... I'll just cry on the stage."
Host: Mariah's hard work and dedication have paid off, and she's been named a Rising Star by Honda Stage and Billboard. She's humbled by the recognition and hopes to live up to her new title.
[Clip of Mariah The Scientist speaking]
Mariah The Scientist: "I said, 'Wow, I'm not under a rock anymore, and maybe all the work, tireless nights and tireless days are getting me somewhere.' I'm just so flattered..."
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for today's episode. Thanks for tuning in to hear from the talented Mariah The Scientist. We can't wait to see what the future holds for this rising star.
